annotations.anchor_quote: I. Introduction Dialogue is the art of exchanging perspectives to resolve differences. It efficient use can transform conflicts into cooperation leading towards the end of forces that create division.
annotations.comment: Introduction section defines dialogue but lacks explicit thesis; add concise stance statement affirming 'dialogue is the antidote of division'.

annotations.anchor_quote: 1. Dialogue enables articulation of needs, fears and expectations.
annotations.comment: Sub-point is merely descriptive lacking analytical depth; explain how articulation leads to resolving divisions via cause-effect.

annotations.anchor_quote: It efficient use can transform conflicts into cooperation leading towards the end of forces that create division.
annotations.correction: Its efficient use can transform conflicts into cooperation leading towards the end of forces that create division.
annotations.comment: Missing possessive 's'; change 'It efficient use' to 'Its efficient use'.

annotations.anchor_quote: Dialogue is the anti-dote of Division
annotations.correction: Dialogue is the antidote of Division
annotations.comment: 'anti-dote' misspelled; correct to 'antidote'.

annotations.anchor_quote: Franco - German dialoque
annotations.correction: Franco-German dialogue
annotations.comment: 'dialoque' misspelled and spacing inconsistent; correct to 'Franco-German dialogue'.

annotations.anchor_quote: In this context, dialogue is not merely idealistic but an indisponsible need for unity and
annotations.comment: Lacks explicit thesis and overview of main arguments; add one-sentence thesis stating stance and previewing structure (e.g., how, impediments, recommendations).
